Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが事務所を去ってから、 ２ヶ月‥‥
English: It's been two months since Maya left the office...

Japanese: ぼくは１度も法廷に立っていない。
English: Two months without a single trial.

Japanese: 依頼はあったけど、なんとなく‥‥ その気になれなかった。
English: I've had offers... But none I took.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。 あの日、彼女が訪ねてくるまでは。
English: That is... until the day that girl showed up.

Japanese: ２月２２日　午前１０時　２分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: February 22, 10:02 AM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: （やれやれ。依頼を引き受ける 気はないのに‥‥）
English: (Why do I come here to the office every day?)

Japanese: （どうして毎日、事務所を 開けてるのかな、ぼく‥‥）
English: (It's not like I want to work...)

Japanese: あー。やっと帰ってきた！
English: There you are! Finally!

Japanese: もう！　どこへ行ってたんですか！
English: Where have you been!?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんの裁判、 もう明日なんですよ！
English: My sister's trial is tomorrow!

Japanese: あ、あの。‥‥キミは？
English: Um... who are you?

Japanese: そんなコトより 今、ダイジなのは！
English: It doesn't matter who I am! It only matters who YOU are!

Japanese: あなたが、弁護士の綾里　千尋さん だというジジツですっ！
English: You're the famous defense attorney, Mia Fey!

Japanese: あ、あれ。もしかして‥‥ ちがいました？
English: Oh, uh... You're not Mia Fey, are you?

Japanese: すみませんが‥‥綾里　千尋は その。もう、ここには‥‥
English: I'm sorry but Ms. Mia Fey no longer... works here.

Japanese: じゃあ、あなたは‥‥ お茶くみのヒト、ですか？
English: So you are...? The coffee boy?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一です。 いちおう、弁護士の。
English: I'm Phoenix Wright... A defense attorney.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど‥‥なるほど‥‥ あッ！
English: Wright... Wright... Wait!
You're THE Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: もしかして、あの！ 御剣検事殺人事件の！
English: The Phoenix Wright from the Edgeworth murder case!?

Japanese: ま、まあ、そんなトコロです。 （御剣は死んでないけど）
English: Um, yes, that's correct. (It wasn't Edgeworth who was murdered, though...)

Japanese: あー、よかった！　この際、 ゼータクは言ってられないし‥‥
English: That's a relief then! You're better than nobody!

Japanese: 悪いけど‥‥今はちょっと、 依頼は受けていないんですよ。
English: I'm sorry... I'm afraid I'm not taking cases right now.

Japanese: え！　でも、成歩堂さんですよね？ 弁護士で。無敵の。
English: But, you are Phoenix Wright, right?
The undefeated defense attorney?

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、今は事件を 引き受けていないんですよ。
English: Look, I'm not accepting any new cases.

Japanese: 申しわけありませんけど。
English: I'm sorry, but you'll have to try elsewhere.

Japanese: おねがいです！ ‥‥もう、時間がないの。
English: Please! I'm out of time!

Japanese: そう言われましても‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: お、お姉ちゃんが‥‥
English: Please, you have to help! I-it's my sister!

Japanese: （‥‥まよい‥‥ちゃん‥‥？）
English: (Maya...? Could it be...?)

Japanese: わかりました。 お話をうかがいましょう。
English: Okay. I'll hear you out.

Japanese: ほ、ホントですかっ！ ありがとうございます！
English: R-really!? Thank you so much!

Japanese: あたし‥‥カガク捜査官の 宝月　茜（ほうづきあかね）です！
English: My name's Ema, Ema Skye. I'm a scientific investigator.

Japanese: （か。かがくそうさかん‥‥？）
English: (Scientific investigator?)

Japanese: 千尋さんが“チャーリー”と 名づけてかわいがっていた植物。
English: Mia's plant, "Charley."

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんがいなくなってからも、 こいつの世話だけは忘れない。
English: I've been taking care of him in Maya's absence.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ちょっと読んでみたら、 アタマが痛くなった。
English: I tried reading one, and it made my head hurt.

Japanese: しまうとき、落っことして 足まで痛くなった。
English: When I closed it, it slipped out of my hand. Then my foot hurt too.

Japanese: 今日も向かいのホテルは 大はんじょうだ。
English: Looks like it's cleaning day again at the hotel across the way.

Japanese: なんでも今度、支店を 出すそうだ。
English: I hear they're planning a second branch outside the city.

Japanese: ‥‥ふと目をやったら、 あのボーイと目が合ってしまった。
English: Egads! The bellboy was staring right at me.

Japanese: 千尋さんが使っていたデスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: 依頼を引き受けなくなってから、 ますます座る機会がへった。
English: I sit here even less now that I've stopped taking cases.

Japanese: ‥‥たまには そうじしないとなあ。
English: I ought to at least dust it off once in a while.

Japanese: トノサマンのポスターが 張ってある。
English: There's a poster of the Steel Samurai on the wall.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが、帰りぎわに 張りかえていったみたいだ。
English: Maya stuck it up here on the day that she left.

Japanese: せっかくの置きみやげだから、 そのままにしてあるんだけど‥‥
English: I didn't have the heart to take it down.

Japanese: たまに、お客さんの視線が 妙にイタいときがある。
English: I do sometimes get strange looks from the clients, though.

Japanese: あかねさん、でしたっけ。 たしか、《科学捜査官》とか‥‥
English: Ema, was it? So you're a "scientific investigator"?

Japanese: ええ！　そうなんです。 それが何か？
English: Yes! That's right! Is... something wrong?

Japanese: それにしてはずいぶん、落ちつきが ‥‥いや、若いなあ、って。
English: No, it's just, you seem kind of, er, jumpy. Or maybe just... young?

Japanese: あ、申しおくれました！ 今年で１６才になります！
English: Young?
I'll be sixteen years old this year!

Japanese: ああ、そうなんですか‥‥ って、ちょっと待って！
English: Oh, I see... wait! Only sixteen!?

Japanese: あと３年もすれば、正式に 鑑識課への配属が決まりますから。
English: I'm set to be formally assigned to Forensics in three more years.

Japanese: あたし、ちょっとしたモンなんです ‥‥すでにこのトシにして、もう。
English: My work is becoming quite well known... At my age, no less!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。現在の職業を セイカクに言うと‥‥？
English: Um, so what exactly is your current position, then?

Japanese: まあ、カガク的にあえてゲンミツに 言うのであれば‥‥
English: Well, legally speaking...

Japanese: “こうこうにねんせい”？ になるのかもしれませんケド。
English: I guess you'd call me an "Eleventh Grader."

Japanese: でも、あたしは！　身もココロも！ すでにこのトシにして、もう！
English: But I'm ready to do my job! At my age, no less!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ タマゴか。この子も）
English: (Great, another future professional in training...)

Japanese: それで‥‥事件、っていうのは？ たしか、裁判は明日だとか。
English: So what's this about a case? You said the trial's tomorrow?

Japanese: あの。お姉ちゃんじゃありません！ ヒトをナイフで刺し殺すなんて‥‥
English: My sister didn't do it! She wouldn't stab someone with a knife! She wouldn't!

Japanese: ‥‥殺人事件、なんだ。
English: So... it's a murder case.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが刺したトコを見た人が いても、カンケーありません！
English: I don't care if there's a witness who saw her do it! She didn't do it!

Japanese: だってあたし、カガク的に 知ってるから！　お姉ちゃんは‥‥
English: I know she didn't do it! It's a scientific fact!

Japanese: ‥‥目撃者がいるんだ。
English: And... there's a witness.

Japanese: と、とにかく！　お姉ちゃんと お話ししてみてください！
English: J-just talk to her! You have to talk to her!

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥ そうさせてもらうよ。
English: Right... I suppose I will.

Japanese: ホントは、綾里　千尋さんを連れて いく、って約束したんですけど。
English: I promised her I'd bring Mia Fey, but...

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (That's interesting...)

Japanese: （どうして知っていたんだろう？ 千尋さんのコト‥‥）
English: (How would she know Mia?)

Japanese: 大きくなったら 科学捜査官になりたいんだ。
English: So, you want to be a scientific investigator when you grow up, then?

Japanese: な、なんですか！ あたし、コドモじゃありません！
English: E-excuse me?
I'm not a child, I'll have you know!

Japanese: それにしても、ずいぶんマニアック ‥‥具体的なユメだね。
English: Still, it's good to have a goal. Albeit a very unusual one.

Japanese: これからの捜査は、もっと科学的に ハンニンを追いつめるべきです！
English: I believe investigations should be done scientifically!

Japanese: ね。そう思いませんか？
English: Don't you?

Japanese: は、はあ。 （目をキラキラさせているぞ‥‥）
English: Uh, yeah. (Sure can't fault her for a lack of enthusiasm.)

Japanese: そうすれば、お姉ちゃんだって きっと‥‥
English: If this case is handled scientifically, I'm sure my sister's name will be cleared!

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥？
English: Your sister...?

Japanese: あたし、いろいろ勉強してますから ‥‥そのうち、見せてあげますね！
English: I've been doing research, you know!

Japanese: 最新のカガク捜査のなんたるかを！
English: I'm developing a new scientific method of case investigation!
I'll show you when I'm done!

Japanese: ‥‥楽しみにしておくよ。
English: I'm looking forward to it.

Japanese: （とにかく、留置所へ行って お姉さんの話を聞いてみるか）
English: (Guess I should get down to the Detention Center and talk to her sister.)

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが言ってたんです。
English: My sister asked for her specifically.

Japanese: アヤサト　チヒロさん‥‥ 学生時代の後輩なんだ、って。
English: Mia Fey... was a few years below her in school.

Japanese: （千尋さんのセンパイ、か）
English: (So she went to the same school as Mia.)

Japanese: いつか、弁護士が必要になったら チヒロさんに‥‥って。
English: She always told me to go to Mia if I ever needed a defense attorney...

Japanese: ムカシ、よく言ってたから。 だから、あたし‥‥
English: And, well... I need one.

Japanese: ちなみに千尋さん、女性だけどね。
English: Um, incidentally, Mia is a woman.

Japanese: チヒロ‥‥あたしもウスウス “ヘンだな”と思ったんですけど。
English: Mia... Yeah, I thought it was a little strange when I saw you, too.

Japanese: なんだか、お姉さんと 仲がいいみたいだね。
English: Well, it's nice of you to help your sister out like this. You must be close.
Well...

Japanese: 今のお姉ちゃん‥‥ アタシ、好きじゃありません。
English: Actually, when she gets like she is now, I kind of hate her.
(Huh?)

Japanese: でも‥‥あたしには、 たったひとりの家族だから。
English: But... But she's my only family.

Japanese: ‥‥“たったひとり”？ あの、ご両親は‥‥
English: Your only family? What about your parents?

Japanese: 小さいころに、交通事故で 亡くなっちゃいました。
English: They died in a car accident when I was little.

Japanese: そう、なんだ‥‥
English: Oh... I'm sorry.

Japanese: ホラ。これが弁護士バッジだよ。
English: See this? It's my Attorney's Badge.

Japanese: ふむふむ。ほおほお。 これがウワサの‥‥
English: Ahh! Well! I've never seen a real one before.

Japanese: （こんなに食いついてくる子は 初めてだな‥‥）
English: (You're the first one who's actually been interested in mine, believe me.)

Japanese: 材質は銀。金メッキが少し はがれてきてますね。
English: Its composition is mostly silver. The gold plating is flaking a bit.

Japanese: （カガク的に分析されてしまった）
English: (She analyzed it. Scientifically...)

Japanese: 硫化物による腐食は、まだみたい。 ５０００円ぐらい、でしょうか。
English: There doesn't appear to be any corrosion due to sulfides. I'd give you $50.00 for it.

Japanese: 悪いけど、売る気はないよ。 ‥‥まだ。
English: Sorry, but it's not for sale. Yet...

Japanese: あの。これ、なんだけど。
English: Here, see this?

Japanese: ふむふむ。やはり弁護士さんは 見せたがり屋である、と。
English: Ahh! I've noticed that defense attorneys have a tendency to want to show people things.

Japanese: （弁護士の生態をカガク的に カンサツされてしまった‥‥）
English: (What is this? A behavioral study of lawyers?)

Japanese: ‥‥すみません、成歩堂さん。
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ん？　なんのコトかな。
English: Huh? About what?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが‥‥その。 あんな感じで。
English: My sister... She's not always like that you know.

Japanese: まさか、また検事さんを弁護する ハメになるとは思わなかったよ。
English: I just never expected to be defending another prosecutor again.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥ムカシは あんなじゃなかったんです。
English: She's changed a lot.

Japanese: やさしくて、いつも笑っていて‥‥ みんなにも、好かれていて。
English: She used to be so gentle, always smiling. Everybody liked her.

Japanese: ‥‥ふうん。 （想像がつかないぞ‥‥悪いけど）
English: I see... (Sorry, but I'm having trouble imagining that.)

Japanese: 何かあったのかな？　お姉さん。
English: What happened to her?

Japanese: ‥‥あたしにも、ハッキリした 理由はわからないんですけど‥‥
English: I don't know for certain myself...

Japanese: ココロ当たりがあるような、 ないような‥‥あるような。
English: I think maybe she... Well, maybe not.

Japanese: （カガク的に割り切れない 事情がありそうだな）
English: (Sounds like there's something there that defies a simple scientific explanation.)

Japanese: とにかく、現場に行ってみよう。 検事局の地下駐車場、だっけ？
English: Let's go check out this underground parking at the Prosecutor's Office, shall we?

Japanese: は‥‥はいッ！
English: O-Okay!

Japanese: ２月２２日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: February 22 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: （どうしたんだろう‥‥？）
English: (Hmm, I wonder what's wrong with Ema?)

Japanese: （あかねちゃん、ここへ来てから 急におとなしくなっちゃったな）
English: (She got quiet all of a sudden as soon as we arrived.)

Japanese: ‥‥看守。 面会は断ったハズですよ。
English: Guard... I thought I told you I didn't want visitors.

Japanese: すすす、す、すみません！ しし、しかし、妹さんがですね‥‥
English: S-s-s-sorry, ma'am! It's j-j-just, your sister...

Japanese: イイワケは聞きません。
English: No excuses!

Japanese: 来月の給与査定‥‥ 楽しみにしておくことです。
English: Or did you not want a raise this year, hmm?

Japanese: ははは、は、はッ！
English: U-u-u-understood, ma'am!

Japanese: （ななな、な、なんだなんだ！）
English: (Wh-wh-wh-what was that all about?)

Japanese: お。お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: H-hi, Lana.

Japanese: ‥‥私のキオクちがい、かしら。
English: Funny.

Japanese: ここへは来ないようにと 言ったつもりでいました。
English: I seem to remember specifically telling you NOT to come here.

Japanese: ‥‥二度と。
English: Perhaps my memory is failing?
L-look...

Japanese: あ、あたしだって。 来たくなかったよ！　‥‥だけど。
English: I didn't want to come here either, okay?

Japanese: 裁判はもう明日だし、 弁護士さんだって、まだ‥‥
English: But your trial's tomorrow and you still don't have a defense attorney!

Japanese: 被告席に立つのは、私。 あなたには、カンケイありません。
English: I'll be the one in court tomorrow. This has nothing to do with you, Ema.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね？ 成歩堂　龍一弁護士。
English: Isn't that right, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥！ ぼくのコト‥‥知ってるんですか。
English: Hey! How do you know me?

Japanese: 千尋さんからうかがっています。 あなたのことは、いろいろ。
English: Mia mentioned you. I've heard... quite a bit.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。 あなたは、どういう‥‥？
English: Er, I'm sorry. What exactly is it that you do...?

Japanese: 宝月　巴（ほうづきともえ）と 申します。
English: My name is Lana, Lana Skye.

Japanese: ‥‥地方検事局・主席検事です。
English: I'm Chief Prosecutor for this district.

Japanese: け、検事‥‥！ 検事さん、なんですか‥‥！
English: Y-you're a prosecutor!?

Japanese: （‥‥こ、この姉妹‥‥ グーゼン、なのか？）
English: (Two sisters... one a lawyer. Could this be a coincidence?)

Japanese: （あかねちゃんに、ともえさん‥‥ どこからどう見ても‥‥）
English: (Ema... Lana... I mean, they're just like...)

Japanese: どうしたんですか？　成歩堂さん。
English: Is something wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: ともえさんの冷気を全身にあびて、 震え上がっている。
English: He's frozen in fear of the frigid Miss Lana.

Japanese: ‥‥ココロなしか、 ぼくもサムくなってきた。
English: I'm feeling a bit chilly myself.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: 最初に申しあげておきます。 ご存じかもしれませんが‥‥
English: There's something you should know from the start.

Japanese: な、なんでしょう。
English: W-which is?

Japanese: 今回の案件では、被疑者は 全面的に容疑を認めています。
English: The suspect in this case has confessed to the crime.
Huh?

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってよ！ “被疑者”って‥‥
English: W-wait! But the suspect... The suspect is...!

Japanese: つまり、真犯人は‥‥この私。 いかがかしら？　成歩堂弁護士‥‥
English: Me. I did it. Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: とにかく‥‥何があったのか、 話していただけませんか？
English: Well... why don't you begin by telling me exactly what happened.

Japanese: 事件が発生したのは、きのうです。 ２月２１日、午後５時１５分。
English: The crime took place yesterday, February 21, at 5:15 PM.

Japanese: ずいぶんハッキリしてますね。
English: That's quite specific.

Japanese: 目撃者の証言です。
English: It was in the witness's deposition.

Japanese: ‥‥私、犯行の瞬間を ハッキリ見られましたので。
English: A witness clearly saw me committing the crime.

Japanese: そ‥‥それはどうも、その。 運が悪かったですね。
English: Uh... My, that was a bit of bad luck, wasn't it?

Japanese: 現場は、地方検事局の地下駐車場。
English: The crime took place in the underground parking lot at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: 遺体は、私の部下のクルマの 後部トランクから発見されました。
English: The body was found in the trunk of my subordinate's car.

Japanese: 検事局‥‥ですか。 （ブカのクルマ、ねえ‥‥）
English: The Prosecutor's Office, huh? (In your subordinate's car trunk? Classy...)

Japanese: この私が、現行犯でタイホされた 場所でもあります。
English: I was arrested on the spot. Caught red-handed, as it were.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (My, my...)

Japanese: それで、亡くなった被害者 というのは‥‥？
English: So, who was the victim?

Japanese: 警察局の捜査官。 いわゆる“刑事”です。
English: An investigator with the Police Department.
I suppose the correct term is "Detective."

Japanese: けいじ‥‥さん。
English: A detective...?

Japanese: 死因は、失血死です。‥‥胸部を ナイフでひと突きしましたので。
English: Death was due to a loss of blood. He was stabbed once in the stomach.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたが、ですか。
English: By... you?

Japanese: 即死にはいたりませんでしたが、 それが致命傷だったようです。
English: Death wasn't immediate, but the wound was fatal.

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: 成歩堂弁護士。もう一度言います。 被害者は、刑事だった‥‥
English: Allow me to repeat myself, Mr. Wright. The victim was a detective.

Japanese: これが何をイミするか？ モチロン、おわかりでしょう？
English: You know what that means, don't you?

Japanese: え！
English: Uh oh!

Japanese: ナニをイミするんですか？ 成歩堂さん！
English: What? Mr. Wright? What does it mean?

Japanese: そ、それは、モチロン、その‥‥
English: Well, it means...

Japanese: 警察は、威信にかけて、有罪判決を “取り”に来る‥‥そういうこと。
English: The police department will consider it a matter of pride to have me found guilty.

Japanese: いかなる手を使っても、私を 追いつめようとするでしょう。
English: They will use any means at their disposal to do so.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥トンでもないコトに なっちまったな‥‥今回も）
English: (This case gets worse and worse with everything I learn.)

Japanese: それで、あなたは‥‥たしか、 《主席検事》とか。
English: So, you're the Chief Prosecutor?

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。
English: That is correct.

Japanese: 地方検事局が抱えている全公判を マクロなカタチで把握して‥‥
English: I'm responsible for overseeing every trial handled by prosecutors in this district.

Japanese: 適切な検事のアテンドと、ケア。 彼らのタスクをつねに管理する‥‥
English: I make sure the prosecutors have what they need to do the job, and manage every aspect.

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンに言えば、 そんな職務でしょうか。
English: Those are my responsibilities, in a nutshell.

Japanese: （ゼッタイ、もっとカンタンに 言えると思うけど‥‥）
English: (That's an awfully large nutshell.)

Japanese: ‥‥それよりも。
English: Still, I'm a little surprised.

Japanese: あなたは、地方検事局の主席検事の カオも知らなかったのですか。
English: I would think you'd recognize the district's Chief Prosecutor, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥あり得ないコト。 そう申し上げるほかありません。
English: In fact, it seems impossible you wouldn't...

Japanese: あの‥‥お姉ちゃん。 その、手の包帯‥‥どうしたの？
English: Um... Lana? What happened to your hand?

Japanese: ああ。これかしら？
English: Oh, this?

Japanese: ちょっと、自分の手を切って しまいましたの‥‥刺すときに。
English: I cut myself by accident. When I stabbed him, that is.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 私も、さすがに殺人には 不慣れなものですから‥‥
English: I'm not very good at being a criminal, I suppose.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。こんなのを 弁護しろってのか‥‥？）
English: (How am I supposed to defend this!?)

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Time to change the subject...)

Japanese: （たしか“センパイ”なんだよな。 ‥‥千尋さんの）
English: (Wait, she was in the class ahead of Mia, wasn't she...?)

Japanese: あの。シツレイですけど‥‥ センパイ、なんですよね？
English: Um, you were in school with Mia, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥綾里　千尋さんの。
English: A few years above her?

Japanese: あかねが、そんなことまで？
English: ... Ema told you that, too, did she?

Japanese: い、いいじゃない！　こうして、 事務所から連れてきたんだから！
English: W-well, why not? I did drag him all the way here from his office!

Japanese: あらゆるイミで、彼女と共通点が あるようには見えませんけど。
English: Although it seems he has very little in common with Mia...

Japanese: （ヨケイなお世話だよ！）
English: (Hey!)

Japanese: ‥‥法科大学時代のことです。 私が３回生、彼女は特別聴講生‥‥
English: It was in law school. I was in my third year, and she was auditing the class.

Japanese: まわりの学生とは、アキラカに フンイキがちがいました。
English: She was different than the other students.

Japanese: フンイキが‥‥
English: Different?

Japanese: 弁護士になるためならば、なんでも やる‥‥そんなハクリョクが。
English: She was strong... She'd do anything to become a defense attorney. Anything.

Japanese: きっと‥‥だから、この私に 近づいてきたのでしょう。
English: That... was probably why she was attracted to me.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: E-excuse me!?

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、主席だったんです。 法科大学で、イチバン‥‥
English: Intellectually attracted! Lana was top of her class in school.

Japanese: 優秀なのです。‥‥ワタクシは。
English: I was the best there was.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Oh...

Japanese: あ。ちなみに、 あたしも優秀ですからっ！
English: I'm doing pretty good in school too, by the way!

Japanese: （姉妹で同じコトを言っても、 ゼンゼンちがって聞こえるな）
English: (It sounds a bit different when Ema says it...)

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、キレイですね。 このバッジ。
English: Your badge looks new.

Japanese: 毎日、みがいてますからね。
English: I polish it daily.

Japanese: 数年もすれば、バッジの金メッキは はがれ落ちるはずです。
English: In a few years, the gold plating will flake off.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとき。きっと、あなたの 本当の姿が見えるでしょう。
English: Then we'll see the real you.

Japanese: （ううう‥‥気軽な世間話が できないのかな、このヒト‥‥）
English: (Gah! What ever happened to innocent small talk!?)

Japanese: あなたの、３年後‥‥ 楽しみにしています。
English: Give it three years. Then we'll see what you have become.

Japanese: あの。これなんですけど‥‥
English: Would you mind taking a look at this?

Japanese: ‥‥１つ、教えてさしあげます。 成歩堂弁護士。
English: I'll say this once and only once, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 弁護士と検事が、法廷外で 証拠品を論じるのは、タブーです。
English: Attorneys and prosecutors have no business showing evidence outside court.

Japanese: たがいに利害のからむ案件ならば、 なおさら‥‥
English: It's taboo. Especially when the interests of both parties are involved.

Japanese: これは、キホンです。 おぼえおいて、ソンはありません。
English: This is a basic principle, Mr. Wright. You'd do well to learn it.

Japanese: 怒られちゃいましたね。
English: She got pretty mad at you.

Japanese: ヒトに説教できる立場じゃ ないと思うけどなあ。
English: You wouldn't think she'd be in the position to lecture anyone right now.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。 いかがかしら、成歩堂弁護士。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: は、はい？
English: E-excuse me?

Japanese: 私はこのとおり、“やった”と 申し上げているのです。
English: As you can plainly see, I am admitting my guilt.

Japanese: この案件を引き受ける‥‥ あり得ない選択、と断言できます。
English: I think it's safe to say... there's no way you can take this case. None.

Japanese: お、お姉ちゃん！
English: B-but, Lana!

Japanese: いつも、そう‥‥
English: You... you were always this way, weren't you?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんは、いつも 自分のコトしか考えてないんだ。
English: You never think of anyone but yourself.

Japanese: あたし、知ってるのに。 お姉ちゃんは、やってない、って。
English: I know you didn't do it, Lana, I know!

Japanese: なのに‥‥ どうして、そんなコトを言うの！
English: So... So how can you say you did!?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんがいなくなったら‥‥
English: If I lose you...

Japanese: あたし、ひとりぼっちに なっちゃうんだよ‥‥？
English: I'll be all alone!

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥ お姉ちゃんなんて、キライ！ I...
English: I hate you, Lana!

Japanese: 成歩堂弁護士。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。
English: Y-yes?

Japanese: どうやら、この面会も ここまで、のようですね。
English: I believe our discussion here is ended.

Japanese: あとは‥‥ あなたにお任せいたします。
English: The rest... I leave to you.

Japanese: それって、つまり‥‥ 弁護の依頼、ということですか？
English: Um... you mean, you're requesting my services as your defense?

Japanese: むずかしく考えないコト。 依頼人は、罪を認めているのです。
English: Don't lose any sleep over it. Your client has confessed, after all. The case is over.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 ぼくなりに、調べてみましょう。
English: Right... I'll do what I can to get to the bottom of this.

Japanese: （ともえさんは、罪を認めている。 しかし‥‥）
English: (Lana has confessed to the crime, yes...)

Japanese: （１つだけ、どうしても 気になるコトがあった）
English: (But something doesn't fit.)

Japanese: （あかねちゃんの、あの目‥‥）
English: (It's that look in Ema's eyes.)

Japanese: （何か‥‥この事件にはまだ、 見えていない部分がある‥‥！）
English: (There's something else going on here... and I'm going to find out what!)

Japanese: ２月２２日　某時刻 上級検事執務室・１２０２号
English: February 22 High Prosecutor's Office Room 1202

Japanese: ‥‥シャレてますね。
English: This... this is quite the place.

Japanese: うん。ニクらしいほどに。
English: You can say that again.

Japanese: でも。でも。成歩堂さんだって、 これぐらいできるでしょ？
English: But, you know Mr. Wright, you could do your office up like this too!

Japanese: おカネとセンスさえあれば。
English: All you need is money and a little design sense...

Japanese: ‥‥両方とも、 自信のない分野なんだよなあ。
English: I'm not doing so well in either of those areas.

Japanese: とにかく、おルスみたいですね。 ここの検事さん。
English: In any case, it looks like the prosecutor is out.

Japanese: また、あとで来てみようか。
English: Let's come back later.

Japanese: なんだ、コレ。《盾》みたい だけど‥‥コワれてるぞ。
English: What's this? It looks like a shield of some kind... It's broken.

Japanese: チョコレートでできていて、 少しカジったのかな。
English: Maybe it's made out of chocolate, and he took a bite out of it.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ブロンズ製に 金メッキ、ですね。
English: It appears to be made out of bronze with gold plating.

Japanese: 高めに見つもっても‥‥ ７０００円ぐらい、かな。
English: It's not worth much. $70 tops.

Japanese: （何かと値段を つけたがるな、この子は）
English: (This girl has a thing about pricing everything, doesn't she?)

Japanese: 《Ｋ》の文字が、なんとなく ナゾめいていますよねー‥‥
English: I wonder what that big "K" means? Mysterious...

Japanese: ひゃあ！
English: Whoa!

Japanese: すごいながめですねー。 １２階でしたっけ。ここ。
English: What a view! It must be nice to have an office on the 12th floor.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥なんか、 エラくなった気分になるね。
English: I guess you would feel... important.

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに。
English: Incidentally...

Japanese: ここから飛び降りたとき、地面に 激突するまでの時間は‥‥
English: Were you to jump out this window, the time until impact with the ground would be...

Japanese: 出ましたっ！ 約３．２３秒ですっ！
English: Got it! Approximately 3.23 seconds!

Japanese: （‥‥だから、なんなんだ）
English: (That's handy to know...)

Japanese: シゴト机だ。さすが、 キレイに片づいているなあ。
English: A work desk. It's quite tidy, as one might expect.

Japanese: いいですねー、この机。 使いやすいけど、オシャレで。
English: What a nice desk! Easy to use, and easy on the eyes!

Japanese: カオが映るほど、よーく みがきこんでありますよ。
English: It's polished so high I can see my own reflection.

Japanese: ‥‥自分のカオを見つめながら シゴトをしたくないなあ。
English: I'd prefer not to have to look at myself while I work...

Japanese: うわあ‥‥これ、ゼンブ 事件のファイルですか！
English: Whoa! These are all case files!?

Japanese: 天井まで、ギッシリ詰まってます！ ハシゴもついていますね。
English: They're stacked up to the ceiling! There's even a ladder...

Japanese: ‥‥でも。こんなにギッシリ 詰まっていたら‥‥
English: With them already packed in so tight like that...

Japanese: 新しい事件のファイルが増えたら どうするつもりなんだろ。
English: what's he going to do when he gets new case files?

Japanese: さすが、成歩堂さん！ 着眼点がザンシンですよね！
English: Splendid, Mr. Wright! What an unusual observation!

Japanese: （ホメられた‥‥）
English: (I think that was a compliment...)

Japanese: あ。チェスボードがありますよ！
English: Hey, a chessboard!

Japanese: チェスはよくわからないけど‥‥ 追いつめられてるね。青いコマが。
English: I'm not too up on my chess but it looks like Blue's in a bit of a tight spot.

Japanese: 赤いナイトたちが、よってたかって 青いポーンを取りかこんでいます。
English: The Red Knights have surrounded the Blue Pawn...

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？
English: Huh?

Japanese: “ナイト”は“剣を持った騎士” “ポーン”は“歩兵”です。
English: Those horses are mounted knights. Their swords have really sharp "edges!"
And check out that poor pawn, his head is kind of spiky... Kind of reminds me of you.

Japanese: （追いつめるのなら、ふつう “王様”じゃないのかな‥‥？）
English: (Yeah, I know, but isn't the point to surround the King?)

Japanese: きゃあ、カワイイ！ ステキなティーセットですよ！
English: Oooh! Cute! What a pretty tea set!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼく、ティーバッグ派だから。
English: I go more for the instant tea bags myself.

Japanese: すごい！　下のタナに、茶葉と缶が ギッシリ詰まってます！
English: Amazing! The drawer below is filled with packets of tea leaves!

Japanese: 産地別にキチンと並んでますよ。 フレバリーも、種類ごとに！
English: They're all sorted by place of origin and flavor!

Japanese: お。このロイヤル、ミゴトなまでに 暴力的なブレンディングです！
English: Look at this Royal blend! What an exquisitely splendid concoction!

Japanese: （‥‥シュミって、なんでも 奥が深いものなんだなあ‥‥）
English: (There's such a thing as taking a hobby too far...)

Japanese: わあ。ゴーカな花ですねェ。
English: Wow! What an amazing bouquet!

Japanese: たしかにね。‥‥あ。 カードが添えてあるぞ。
English: No kidding. Hey, there's a card on it...

Japanese: ‥‥《起死回生　　　カオル》
English: "Back from the Dead -- Wendy"

Japanese: （“カオル”‥‥ どこかで聞いたような）
English: ("Wendy?"... I've heard that name somewhere before.)

Japanese: となりにあるのは‥‥ 巨大なトノサマンですっ！
English: And here beside it... A giant Steel Samurai!

Japanese: うわ、ほしいなあ。
English: Wow, I want one!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。足のウラ、 何か書いてあるぞ。
English: Huh? There's something written on the bottom of his foot.

Japanese: ‥‥《青息吐息　　　カオル》
English: "Between a rock and a hard place. -- Wendy"

Japanese: この部屋の検事さんの名前‥‥ “カオル”さん、なのかな。
English: Is the prosecutor in this office named "Wendy"?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、ちがうと思うよ。
English: Um... I don't think so.

Japanese: きゃあ、スゴくふかふかで キモチいいですよ！
English: Ooh! What a soft and comfy sofa!

Japanese: 座った瞬間、まさに “ソファ～”って感じです！
English: It makes you want to say, "sofa, so good!"

Japanese: “ソファ～”って感じ。
English: "Sofa"... "So far"...?

Japanese: いちおう、聞こえてるよ。 （ビミョーなコトを言う子だな）
English: Yeah, I got it. (I'm glad she's not an aspiring comedienne...)

Japanese: が‥‥額の中に 服がかざってありますよ！
English: There's a jacket framed on the wall like a painting!

Japanese: イタリア風の仕立て、最高級の カシミアビーバー織の服地。
English: The fabric is high-grade cashmere... Italian styling...

Japanese: シルクのヒラヒラに、トルコ石の 飾りボタンと金糸のエリかざり。
English: Silk ruffles, turquoise buttons, and a gold thread collar.

Japanese: まあ‥‥５０万円ぐらい、 でしょうか。
English: I'm guessing it's worth around $5,000.

Japanese: ごご、ごじゅうまん。
English: F-five thousand dollars!?

Japanese: ちなみに、成歩堂さんの スーツは‥‥
English: Speaking of which, your suit would be about...

Japanese: い、いいよ！ そんな目で見ないでほしいな。
English: Th-that's enough of that. I don't need my life appraised thank you very much.

Japanese: ２月２２日　某時刻 上級検事執務室・１２０２号
English: February 22 High Prosecutor's Office Room 1202

Japanese: いかにも“シゴトできます”って 感じの部屋ですよねー。
English: This is the kind of room that just screams "I can do the job."

Japanese: 成歩堂さんの事務所とちがって。
English: Quite a change from your office, really.

Japanese: ‥‥うるさいな。
English: Thanks.

Japanese: ホラ。ホラ。なんか、トロフィー みたいなのがありますよ！
English: Look, look! There's a trophy or something here!

Japanese: （トロフィー‥‥って、 あの《盾》か‥‥）
English: (A trophy? What, that shield?)

Japanese: こんなの、うれしそうに かざっちゃって。
English: It takes real nerve to display stuff like this.

Japanese: きっと、権力をハナにかけた イヤらしいヤツに決まってますよ！
English: Whoever's office this is, he must be a real stuck-up jerk!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: どこまでも、おせっかいな オトコだな、キミは。
English: You never tire of prying into other people's business, do you?

Japanese: （‥‥この声は‥‥やっぱり）
English: (That voice...!)

Japanese: ひさしぶり‥‥だな、御剣。
English: Long time, no see, Edgeworth.
Huh?

Japanese: あああああああああッ！ み、み、御剣検事さん！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaah! M-M-Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥！ 知ってるの？　コイツのコト。
English: You know him from somewhere?

Japanese: え、え‥‥だってあたし、 大ファンなんです！
English: O-of course! I'm his biggest fan!

Japanese: お姉ちゃんに紹介されたコトも あるし‥‥いろいろと‥‥
English: My sister introduced us once, and....

Japanese: （そうか‥‥この子、 主席検事の妹さんだもんな）
English: (Right... her sister was the Chief Prosecutor, after all.)

Japanese: それで？　なんの用かな。
English: Well? What brings you here?

Japanese: イヤミで権力をハナにかけた イヤらしい検事の部屋に‥‥
English: I'll warn you... I've been known to be a real stuck-up jerk...

Japanese: え！　あ、いえッ！ それは、このヒトが勝手に‥‥
English: N-no! Did I...? No!
It was just, Mr. Wright here, he...

Japanese: いやいや！ ぼくのせいにするなよ。
English: Hey! Don't blame me!

Japanese: あの。あの。あたしたちはただ、 殺人事件を調べてるだけなんです！
English: W-we're just here to investigate a murder case!

Japanese: ‥‥殺人事件‥‥？
English: Murder...?

Japanese: ホラ。イヤミでハデで、まっ赤な クルマから、死体が見つかった‥‥
English: A body was found in this nasty, bright red sports car in the parking lot...

Japanese: むう‥‥
English: Hmm?

Japanese: アレなら、私のクルマだが。 ‥‥それが、なにか？
English: That would be my car. What of it?

Japanese: うえええええええええええええッ！ み、み、み、御剣検事さんのォォ！
English: Whaaaaaaaaaaaa!? Y-y-y-your car!?

Japanese: （いっそ、キモチいいぐらいの 驚きっぷりだなあ‥‥）
English: (I'll say one thing, she certainly can scream.)

Japanese: ‥‥あのおッ！
スミマセン！　御剣検事ってヒト、 いらっしゃるでありますかッ！
English: Excuse me! But is Mr. Edgeworth, uh... anywhere on the premises?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうか。 私が御剣だが。
English: I'm Edgeworth. What is it?

Japanese: 本官！　警察局長のお使いで本官！ 報告書を持ってきたでありますッ！
English: I'm here, sir, at the request of the Chief, sir! I've got your report, sir!

Japanese: 報告書、だと‥‥？
English: Report?

Japanese: まさか！　宝月主席検事の件で、 新しい手がかりが出たのかッ！
English: What? Did you find new evidence in the case against Chief Prosecutor Skye!?

Japanese: （‥‥目の前で、 イヤなハナシをされてるなあ）
English: (I don't like the way this conversation is going at all...)

Japanese: ほーづき、でありますかッ？ ‥‥いえッ！
English: Er... Skye, sir? No, sir!

Japanese: そんな名前、本官！ 報告書にはありませんけれどもッ！
English: No name of that kind, sir! Not in this report, sir!

Japanese: （うわあ‥‥御剣のカンニン袋の ヒモが切れる音が聞こえたぞ）
English: (I think I just heard Edgeworth's lid blow.)

Japanese: ゆ、ユルいですね‥‥ 御剣検事さんのカンニン袋。
English: Mr. Edgeworth's lid isn't on very tight, is it?

Japanese: 警察局には、言っておいた ハズではないかッ！
English: I made a clear request to the Police Department, did I not?

Japanese: 明日の審理に集中したいから、 よけいな書類は届けないように！
English: I need to focus on the trial tomorrow, so don't bring me anything unrelated!

Japanese: はッ！　でも。 でも、だって本官ッ！
English: Sir! But, but sir!

Japanese: コレを持って行けって言われた だけでありますからしてッ！
English: I'm just following orders, sir! They told me to bring this to you!

Japanese: ムズカシイことはコレ本官、 わからないのでありますからし
English: I wasn't aware of the particulars of your arrangement with us, sir!

Japanese: ‥‥キサマの名前はッ！
English: Give me your name!

Japanese: あ、あのッ！　本官、あのッ！ は、は。は。ハラバイです本官ッ！
English: U-uh, yes... yes, Sir! M-M-Meekins, sir. Officer Meekins!

Japanese: もういい、原灰巡査。 報告書を持って、帰りたまえ。
English: Right. Officer Meekins? Take your report and leave.

Japanese: ‥‥来月の給与査定を 楽しみにしておくことだ。
English: And good luck with that raise next month.

Japanese: ううう‥‥ だって本官‥‥そんな、本官‥‥
English: *whimper* B-but, sir, I d-didn't know!

Japanese: （いるよなー。こういう、 アタマも運もよくないヒト‥‥）
English: (Poor guy. Looks like he was absent on the day they gave out brains and good luck.)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。
English: Wright.

Japanese: は、はいッ！　本官！ （こういうときのコイツ、怖ェな）
English: Y-yes, sir!? (Gah! He caught me off guard!)

Japanese: 見てのとおり、私もヒマではない。
English: As you can see, I'm busy.

Japanese: ‥‥キミたちもそろそろ、 帰ったほうがいいだろう。
English: You may leave now.

Japanese: そ、そうしましょうよ。 成歩堂さん！
English: L-let's do what he says, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 被害者は、捜査官‥‥今の 巡査と同じく、警察の人間だ。
English: The victim was a detective from the same department as that patrolman just now.

Japanese: 警察局へ行ってみることだ。 被害者のことは、そこで聞ける。
English: Go down to the Police Department. You can ask more there.

Japanese: あ、ああ。‥‥ありがとう。 （キゲンが悪くなったみたいだな）
English: U-uh... Thanks. (He seems to have finally calmed down at least.)

Japanese: やっぱり、おまえのクルマ だったのか‥‥あれ。
English: So, the body was found in your car?

Japanese: フッ‥‥思ったままを 言えばいいだろう、成歩堂。
English: Go ahead, say it, Wright.

Japanese: 去年、せっかく 助けてやったオトコが‥‥
English: You think I did it, don't you?

Japanese: ついに、本当に殺人に 手を染めたか、と。
English: After you went through all that trouble to help me last year, no less.

Japanese: そ、そんな！ 御剣検事さんじゃありません！
English: N-no, we don't think you did it!

Japanese: だって、刺したのは あたしのお姉ちゃん‥‥
English: I mean, it was my sister who stabbed him...

Japanese: アレ。ええと、そうじゃなくて ‥‥その。
English: Uh, wait, no, she didn't do that! I mean...
Wait...

Japanese: あなたは、たしか‥‥ 主席検事の妹さん、でしたね。
English: So you're the Chief Prosecutor's little sister, then?

Japanese: は、はい！　宝月　茜ですっ！ ‥‥えーと、あの。
English: Y-yes, sir! Ema Skye! It, uh...

Japanese: そのセツは、ホントにどうも！
English: It's nice to meet you again!

Japanese: （とってつけたような アイサツだな‥‥）
English: (Now that didn't sound forced at all...)

Japanese: ああ‥‥そうですね。 すっかり、見ちがえました。
English: Ah, now I remember. You've... really grown.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、 この私もさすがに予想外だった。
English: I'll admit, it was a surprise to me, too.

Japanese: まさか、自分のクルマで 殺人事件が起きようとは。
English: To think that my own car would become the scene of a murder.

Japanese: しかも‥‥
English: More surprising still...

Japanese: この手で、自分の上司の罪を 立証することになろうとは。
English: Now I'm forced to prove my superior's guilt.

Japanese: ま。そうだろうなあ‥‥ な、なんだって！
English: I can understand... W-wait!... What did you say!?

Japanese: 宝月　巴‥‥主席検事といえば、 地方検事局のトップだ。
English: Lana Skye is the Chief Prosecutor... the top prosecutor in the district.

Japanese: ‥‥明日の法廷は、 この私が担当することになった。
English: She can't prosecute herself, so I'll be the prosecutor at the trial tomorrow.

Japanese: そ、そんな！ 御剣検事さんが‥‥
English: You!? Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: 正直なトコロ‥‥
English: To be honest...

Japanese: ここにこうしていられるのが、 フシギなくらいだ。
English: It's a bit of a miracle I'm still here at all.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだ？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: ウワサ、だ。
English: Rumors.

Japanese: キミも聞いたことがあるだろう。 私についてのウワサは。
English: You've heard the rumors about me, haven't you?

Japanese: （御剣　怜侍‥‥）
English: (Miles Edgeworth...)

Japanese: （たしかに、その名声の影では 黒いウワサがウズを巻いている）
English: (It's hard to remember a time when there WEREN'T rumors about this guy...)

Japanese: （証拠品のねつ造や、偽証の取引、 違法な裏づけ捜査、など‥‥）
English: (Forging evidence, arranging false testimony, illegal searches, you name it...)

Japanese: 年末の、あの事件。キミのおかげで 無実が立証されたが‥‥
English: Thanks to you, my innocence was established in the trial at the end of last year.

Japanese: 今回の事件が、私のしわざである というウワサも流れているようだ。
English: However, there are some who say I'm the one responsible for the current incident.

Japanese: ば、バカ言うなよ‥‥
English: Wh-what? That's crazy!

Japanese: フッ‥‥
English: Hmph.

Japanese: ヒトの悪意というものは、ふとした キッカケであふれ出す。
English: Some people need very little excuse to think ill of others.

Japanese: それを止めることは、 不可能なのだよ。
English: It's a fact of life. Impossible to stop.

Japanese: 御剣検事さん‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: その一方で、こんなオモチャを 私に押しつけるヤカラもいる。
English: Some of them even go so far as to present me with toys like this...

Japanese: ‥‥コッケイ、だな。
English: They think it's funny.

Japanese: （ブロンズの盾‥‥か。 何かいわくがあるのかな）
English: (Toys? That bronze shield? There's got to be a story behind that one...)

Japanese: 宝月主席検事とは、‥‥２年前。 初めていっしょにシゴトをした。
English: Chief prosecutor Skye? Yes...We first worked together on a case two years ago.

Japanese: あれは、私にとっても 初めての大きな事件だった。
English: It was my first big case.

Japanese: ‥‥そう、でしたね。
English: That's right, I remember.

Japanese: （２年前‥‥ぼくはまだ 弁護士にもなってないな）
English: (Two years ago... I wasn't even a lawyer yet.)

Japanese: それ以来、何かと目をかけてくれて いる‥‥そう思っていたのだが。
English: Since then, I always felt that she was looking out for me...

Japanese: どうやら、 私の思いちがいだったようだ。
English: It appears I was mistaken.

Japanese: お、“思いちがい”って、 どうしてですか！
English: M-mistaken!? Why?

Japanese: たしかに、お姉ちゃんは あんな冷たいヒトだけど。
English: I mean, I know she's not the warmest person...

Japanese: きっと、御剣検事さんのコトは‥‥
English: But I'm sure she felt some responsibility for you!

Japanese: ならば‥‥なぜ。
English: Then... why?

Japanese: 彼女は、私のクルマのトランクで ヒトを刺したのだろうか？
English: Why did she stab someone in the trunk of my car?

Japanese: しかも‥‥ この私のナイフを使って。
English: Not only that... She stabbed him with my knife.

Japanese: ‥‥え！　ええええええッ！ 御剣検事さんの‥‥ナイフ！
English: Wha... Whaaaaaat!? Mr. Edgeworth! Your knife was the murder weapon!?

Japanese: 事件の凶器は、トランクの 私の道具箱に入っていたナイフだ。
English: To be specific, it was the knife kept in the toolbox in the trunk of my car.

Japanese: 《御剣のナイフ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Edgeworth's Knife added to the Court Record.

Japanese: あのさ‥‥御剣。
English: Um... Edgeworth?

Japanese: なんだ。
English: What?

Japanese: もしかして、おまえ‥‥ やったんじゃないの？
English: Are you sure you didn't do it?

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。ジョークの センスがないヤツだよ）
English: (C'mon, can't he take a joke?)

Japanese: センスがないのは 成歩堂さんのほうですッ！
English: You have a strange sense of humor, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 事件当日‥‥きのうのこと、 詳しく聞かせてくれないか？
English: Could you tell me more about yesterday... the day of the murder?

Japanese: きのうは‥‥検事局にとって、 年に一度の大そうじがあったのだ。
English: Yesterday was the annual cleaning day at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: 大そうじ‥‥ですか？
English: Cleaning day?

Japanese: 警察局と協力して、決着のついた 事件の証拠やデータの整理をする。
English: Working with the Police Department, we sort and file all evidence for solved cases.

Japanese: 我々は、証拠品の《申し送り》と 呼んでいるが。
English: We call it "evidence transferal."

Japanese: ふむふむ。たしかに “大そうじ”みたいですね‥‥
English: Wiping your hands of old cases, in other words.

Japanese: そして、もう１つ。 警察局で、式典が行われた。
English: Oh, and another thing... A ceremony was held at the Police Department.

Japanese: 今年度の検事・捜査官たちの評価が 発表、そして表彰されたのだ。
English: There's an annual review and awards for outstanding police officers and prosecutors.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。それでこの《盾》を もらった、というワケか。
English: And that's when you got the shield?

Japanese: きのうの午後は、警察局にいた。 戻ってきたのは‥‥５時１２分だ。
English: I was at the police department yesterday afternoon... I got back here at 5:12.

Japanese: ‥‥イヤにハッキリ覚えているな。
English: That's... very precise.

Japanese: 御剣検事さんとあたしは、 成歩堂さんとはちがいますからね。
English: People like myself and Mr. Edgeworth pride ourselves on our precision, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥いや。私はキオクを アテにしないことにしている。
English: No, I place little faith in my memory.

Japanese: このとおり、 キチンと証拠があるのだよ。
English: The only thing I trust is solid evidence.

Japanese: 《御剣の駐車記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Edgeworth's Parking Stub added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 地下駐車場が発行している、 利用記録だ。
English: This is the parking stub from the underground lot.

Japanese: （事件が起こったのは、 ５時１５分ごろ‥‥） 5:15...)
English: (The murder took place around

Japanese: クルマで戻ってすぐに 事件は起こったワケかあ。
English: So the murder happened right after you got back.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ？　成歩堂。 そのシツレイな目は‥‥
English: What, Wright?
I'd appreciate it if you'd direct that suspicious glare elsewhere.

Japanese: 今は、明日の準備で忙しい。
English: I'm busy preparing for tomorrow.

Japanese: そしてそれ以上に、ムダな おしゃべりをする気分ではない。
English: And I'm not in the mood for idle banter.

Japanese: ‥‥相手がキミとなれば、 なおさらだ。
English: Especially not with you.

Japanese: か、帰りましょう！ 成歩堂さん！
English: L-let's go, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥キゲンが なおるのを待つとするか）
English: (Fine. I'll just have to talk to him when he perks up.)

Japanese: さっきから気になってたんだけど ‥‥なんだ、コレ。
English: I've been wondering... what the heck is this?

Japanese: 《Ｋ》って書いてありますね。
English: It has a big "K" on it.

Japanese: ‥‥オブ・ザ・イヤー‥‥
English: *mumbles* of Prosecutors...

Japanese: え？　なんだって？
English: Huh? What's that?

Japanese: 《検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー》だ。
English: It's the "King of Prosecutors" trophy.

Japanese: けけ、け。け‥‥ けんじ・おぶ・ざ・いやー！
English: K-K-K... "King of Prosecutors"!?

Japanese: その年度でイチバン優秀な検事に 贈られる、名誉ある像だ。
English: It's a great honor. They send that shield to the best prosecutor each year.

Japanese: ‥‥悪いかッ！
English: What! So?

Japanese: も‥‥もしかして、 この《Ｋ》って‥‥
English: So that "K"... that's...

Japanese: “けんじ”の《Ｋ》ですか？
English: "K" stands for "King"?

Japanese: うるさいッ！
English: Yeah, you got a problem with that?

Japanese: 私がデザインしたワケでは ないのだッ！
English: I didn't design the thing!

Japanese: ひゃああ。ちょっと 恥ずかしいですねー、コレは。
English: "King of Prosecutors"... Kind of like "Employee of the Month," only better!

Japanese: 《検事局賞の盾》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: King of Prosecutors Trophy added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 検事・オブ・ザ・イヤーの 賞品ですね。《盾》のカタチの。
English: So that's the "King of Prosecutors" shield, huh?

Japanese: よかったな、御剣。 検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー。
English: Well done, Edgeworth. You must be proud to be the King of Prosecutors!

Japanese: おめでとうございます！ 検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー。
English: Congratulations! King! Of! Prosecutors!

Japanese: たのむから‥‥何度も 言わないでもらえるだろうか。
English: Please, stop saying that.

Japanese: （それにしても‥‥）
English: (That still doesn't explain one thing...)

Japanese: （この盾、なんで先っぽが 欠けているんだろう‥‥）
English: (Why is the tip of this shield broken off?)

Japanese: シゴト机だ。さすが、 キレイに片づいているなあ。
English: A work desk. It's quite tidy, as one might expect.

Japanese: いいですねー、この机。 使いやすいけど、オシャレで。
English: What a nice desk! Easy to use, and easy on the eyes!

Japanese: カオが映るほど、よーく みがきこんでありますよ。
English: It's polished so well I can see my own reflection.

Japanese: （‥‥なぜだろう。イトノコ刑事の カオが浮かんだぞ‥‥）
English: (Strange... Why did I just picture Detective Gumshoe?)

Japanese: あのネームプレート、おみやげに 持って帰ろうかなあ。
English: Maybe I'll take that name plaque as a souvenir.

Japanese: やめたほうがいいよ。起訴される。
English: Don't. He'll sue you.

Japanese: うわあ‥‥これ、ゼンブ 事件のファイルですか！
English: Whoa! These are all case files!?

Japanese: 天井まで、ギッシリ詰まってます！ ハシゴもついていますね。
English: They're stacked up to the ceiling! There's even a ladder...

Japanese: ‥‥おかしいな。アイツ、 地震がニガテなはずだから‥‥
English: Odd...

Japanese: こんな危なっかしい本棚を 使うとは思えないんだけど。
English: I thought Edgeworth wasn't good with heights.

Japanese: だいじょうぶです！
English: He must have someone get them for him.

Japanese: ものすごく大きなボルトで、カベに メリこむほど固定されています！
すごいパワーだなあ。 御剣検事さんがやったのかなあ。
（‥‥なぜだろう。イトノコ刑事の カオが浮かんだぞ‥‥）
English: (Strange... Why did I just picture Detective Gumshoe?)

Japanese: でもさ。地震が起こったら、 部屋中にファイルの雨が降るね。
だいじょうぶです！
English: He must study these case reports so closely... He's so cool!

Japanese: ギュウギュウに詰まってますから、 ちょっとやそっとじゃ抜けません！
やっぱり、なんか おかしくない？　それ。
English: You wouldn't say that if you saw him sweating bullets up on that ladder.

Japanese: あ。チェスボードがありますよ！
English: Hey, a chessboard!

Japanese: チェスはよくわからないけど‥‥ 追いつめられてるね。青いコマが。
English: I'm not too up on my chess but it looks like Blue's in a bit of a tight spot.

Japanese: 赤いナイトたちが、よってたかって 青いポーンを取りかこんでいます。
English: The Red Knights have surrounded the Blue Pawn...

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？
English: Huh?

Japanese: “ナイト”は“剣を持った騎士” “ポーン”は“歩兵”です。
English: Those horses are mounted knights. Their swords have really sharp "edges!"
And check out that poor pawn, his head is kind of spiky... Kind of reminds me of you.

Japanese: 赤い“剣”が、青い“歩”を 追いつめている‥‥
御剣検事さん、チェス好きかあ。 ‥‥どうしました？　成歩堂さん。
English: Mr. Edgeworth must be an avid chess player! What's wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （気のせいだよな、きっと）
English: (Edges... surrounding a pawn with spiky hair... Nah... It's nothing.)

Japanese: わあ。ゴーカな花ですねェ。 御剣検事さんに、ピッタリ！
English: My, my, my! What an amazing bouquet! Just right for Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: たしかにね。‥‥あ。 カードが添えてあるぞ。
English: No kidding. Hey, there's a card on it...

Japanese: ‥‥《起死回生　　　カオル》
English: "Back from the Dead -- Wendy"

Japanese: （“カオル”‥‥ どこかで聞いたような）
English: ("Wendy?"... I've heard that name somewhere before.)

Japanese: となりにあるのは‥‥ 巨大なトノサマンですっ！
English: And beside it... A giant Steel Samurai!

Japanese: うわ、ほしいなあ。 御剣検事さん、マニアなのかなあ。
English: Wow, I want one!

Japanese: イヤだな、それ。‥‥あ。 足のウラ、何か書いてあるぞ。
English: Huh? There's something written on the bottom of his foot.

Japanese: ‥‥《青息吐息　　　カオル》
English: "Between a rock and a hard place -- Wendy"

Japanese: カオルさんって‥‥御剣検事さんの フィアンセ、ですか？
English: Wendy? Is she Mr. Edgeworth's fiancee?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、ちがうと思うよ。
English: Um... I don't think so.

Japanese: さすが、御剣検事さん。 ふかふかですよ、ソファ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth has such a comfy sofa!

Japanese: こんなリッパなソファ、 座った瞬間に眠くなるよ。
English: Sofas like this make me want to curl up and take a nap.

Japanese: きっと、夜おそくまで 資料を調べて、つかれきって‥‥
English: I bet he pours over his case files here until the wee hours of the morning...

Japanese: 上着をぬいで、シャツのソデなんか オシャレにまくっちゃって。
English: Then he takes off his jacket, rolls up his sleeves...

Japanese: ウデまくらで、ついウトウト 寝ちゃうんですよねー。
English: And goes to sleep using his arms as a pillow!

Japanese: （彼女の目には、見えている らしいな‥‥オソロシイことに）
English: (I don't believe it. She's actually daydreaming about Edgeworth working...)

Japanese: 寝グセや、ホッペにソファの カドのアトがついちゃって。
English: I bet in the morning he has sofa hair, and little creases in his cheek from the seams!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいなあ。
English: He's so cool!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいかあ？
English: Sofa-hair is cool!?

Japanese: うわ！　なんか、いちだんと ヒラヒラした服ですよ！
English: Wow! This jacket is even lacier than his usual ones!

Japanese: いわゆる“勝負服”かな。 御剣検事さんの！
English: This must be his lucky trial jacket!

Japanese: 勝負服、ねえ‥‥。 ぼくは見たことないけどな。
English: Lucky jacket, right... I've never seen him wear it.

Japanese: 額に入れてかざっちゃって。 ナニか“いわく”がありそう！
English: I'm sure there's a story behind why it's in a frame!

Japanese: ちょっとシツレイして。 写真、撮っちゃお！
English: Maybe I'll be naughty... and take a picture!

Japanese: （やれやれ。 コーフンぎみだな‥‥）
English: (She's getting way too excited about this.)

Japanese: ‥‥ムカシ、あこがれていた 時期があったものだ。
English: I once dreamed of being a defense attorney, a long time ago.

Japanese: え！　御剣検事さんが、 弁護士に‥‥？
English: What? You wanted to be a defense attorney, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: しかし、進むべき道が目の前に のびている以上‥‥
English: Yet, my path is laid out clearly before me...

Japanese: 今さら振りかえるわけには いかないのだろうな。
English: I have no time to reflect on what might have been.

Japanese: 御剣、これなんだけど‥‥
English: Say, Edgeworth, I was wondering about this...

Japanese: あ、成歩堂さん！
English: M-Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ん？　なに？
English: Huh? What?

Japanese: そんなモノ、御剣検事さんに 見せていいんですか？
English: Are you sure you should be showing that to Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥あ。 （カクジツに取りあげられるな）
English: Oh. (He'll take it for sure, won't he.)

Japanese: ‥‥はああ。
English: *sigh*

Japanese: ホントは御剣検事さんのミカタに なりたいんだけど‥‥
English: I wish I could be on the same side as Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが有罪に なっちゃうからなあ‥‥
English: But then my sister would be found guilty!

Japanese: （深ぁいタメ息なんか ついてるぞ‥‥）
English: (If she sighs any deeper I'm going to start getting depressed...)

Japanese: つまり。今年イチバンだった ワケだよな。おまえが。
English: So, in other words, you were the best of the best this year, huh?

Japanese: ‥‥そのニヤニヤ笑いは やめてもらいたいな。
English: You can take that foolish grin elsewhere, Wright.

Japanese: それをもらうために、 きのうはシゴトにならなかった。
English: I lost a day of work to receive that travesty.

Japanese: ‥‥？　どうしてですか。
English: Huh? Why's that?

Japanese: そのコワれた盾を受け取るため、 警察局の式典に出席していたのだ。
English: I had to go to the Police Department ceremony to receive that broken shield.

Japanese: 警察局‥‥？
English: The Police Department?

Japanese: 警察署のトナリにある。署のほうは キミも行ったことがあるだろう。
English: Yes. Right next to the Police Station downtown. You've been there, correct?

Japanese: ああ‥‥イトノコ刑事がいる、 あそこか。
English: Where Detective Gumshoe works? Yeah.

Japanese: あの‥‥この《盾》なんですけど。 なんでコワれてるんですか？
English: Um, I was wondering something about your shield... Why is it broken?

Japanese: 大したイミはない。 ‥‥今となっては、ね。
English: What does it matter? I've got more important things to worry about.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥
English: Oh. Right...

Japanese: （キョーミがないみたいだな、 賞とか、そういうモノには‥‥）
English: (He doesn't seem too concerned about his award, for better or for worse.)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥きのうは、検事局が 最も忙しい１日だったのだ。
English: Yesterday was a very busy day for the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: ‥‥もう少し聞いたほうがいい みたいですね。きのうのコト‥‥
English: Maybe... we should ask him more about yesterday?

Japanese: 弁護士と証拠について 語るシュミはない。
English: It's against my policy to discuss evidence with the defense.

Japanese: ‥‥相手がキミとなれば、 なおさらだ。
English: Especially with you.

Japanese: キラわれてるんですね、 成歩堂さん。
English: He doesn't like you much, does he, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （そういうコトじゃない）
English: (Nah. With Edgeworth it's never personal... It's all about winning tomorrow.)
